Aether Cosmology Research Group

Airy's Failure

The 1871 water-telescope experiment, what it measured, and the frame it forces.
Presented by Alan · Space Audits
Section One

The Measurement

What aberration is, what Airy built, and what the instrument actually reports.
Bradley, 1728

Stars sit slightly off-position

  • Bradley took zenith measurements of γ Draconis as it transited his telescope.
  • Predicted transit position and observed position differed by 20.55″.
  • The displacement is gradual: it accumulates over six months and reverses over the next six.
  • Attributed to the observer's own velocity composing with the velocity of light.
The umbrella

Run through vertical rain and you tilt the umbrella forward. The rain has not changed direction. Your motion has. Aberration is that tilt, applied to starlight.

Aberration is a first-order effect: it scales with v/c, not v²/c².
The stakes

The only first-order measurement

  • Kepler's kinematics and Newton's dynamics derive from one another. The mathematics alone cannot decide the model.
  • Aberration is the one measurement that returns a first-order v/c relationship giving 30 km/s.
  • From 1812 (Arago) to 1887 (Michelson), every further attempt at a first-order measurement returned null.
  • The nulls were explained by ad-hoc aether constructions built to cancel exactly what was being looked for.
The absence of a measurable velocity became the argument for the velocity.
The test

Fill the tube with water

If aberration is the ratio of the observer's velocity to the velocity of light, then slowing the light inside the instrument must change the angle.
tan(aberration) = v / cmedium water: c/n = 0.75 c → predicted 27.33″ internal, 36.35″ read
A clean, falsifiable prediction. Airy built the instrument to test it.
Greenwich, 1871

The instrument

  • A zenith sector: fixed vertical, spirit-levelled, not pointable.
  • Levels checked at every single observation; readings taken in reversed positions.
  • Star position read against a wire plate by micrometer.
  • Observations on selected dates across the year, sampling different points in the orbit.
  • Tube length 37.0 in; the water plate calibrated as a 27.8 in air-equivalent.
FIGURE
Airy zenith sector diagram
A fixed vertical mount matters: the starlight strikes the water surface at the aberration angle, not normally.
Instrumentation

What the micrometer reports

Reading

The celestial arc the telescope reports: the star's sideways drift on the wire plate divided by that telescope's own scale. Airy's 20.55″ is a reading, not a direct angle.

Internal angle θ_int

The physical tilt the ray takes inside the tube. Never measured directly. It is the angle required to produce the observed reading. In water, a 20.55″ reading needs 15.45″ inside.

reading = θ_int × n n = 37.0 / 27.8 = 1.33
The water plate is calibrated n times finer. That scaling is why a smaller internal angle reads the same arc.
The result

Nothing changed

Air telescope
20.55″
measured
Water telescope
20.55″
measured
Wave prediction
36.35″
not observed
Readings six months apart differed by 0.8 arcseconds. The instrument could resolve a 2 to 3 arcsecond difference easily. The predicted change was 15 arcseconds.
The prediction failed by a factor of n², well outside the instrument's resolution.
Section Two

The Explanations

What the physics of 1871 offered, in the words of the men who tested it.
Michelson & Morley, 1887

The wave theory fails outright

"It failed to account for the fact, proved by experiment, that the aberration was unchanged when observations were made with a telescope filled with water. For if the tangent of the angle of aberration is the ratio of the velocity of the Earth to the velocity of light, then, since the latter velocity in water is three-fourths of its velocity in a vacuum, the aberration observed with a water telescope should be four-thirds of its true value."
Michelson, A. A. & Morley, E. W. (1887). On the Relative Motion of the Earth and the Luminiferous Ether. American Journal of Science, 34, 333–345.
Their own crucial-experiment paper opens by stating the classical account is dead on arrival.
Michelson & Morley, 1887 — footnote

Emission theory fails worse

"Most writers admit the sufficiency of the explanation according to the emission theory of light, while in fact the difficulty is even greater than according to the undulatory theory. For, on the emission theory, the velocity of light must be greater in the water telescope, and therefore the angle of aberration should be less. Hence, in order to reduce it to its true value, we must make the absurd hypothesis that the motion of the water in the telescope carries the ray of light in the opposite direction."
Michelson & Morley (1887), footnote to p. 333.
The man who falsified emission theory calls its rescue absurd, in print.
Where things stood in 1887

Every theory, one table

Treatment of lightSpeed in waterθ_intReadsMatches
Emission (corpuscular)1.33 c15.45″20.55″angle yes, speed impossible
Undulatory / Snell0.75 c27.33″36.35″no
Klinkerfues 18670.75 c27.33″36.35″no
Water and Earth at rest0.75 c15.45″20.55″yes
In 1871 there was no coherent account of Airy inside a moving-Earth model.
Section Three

The Transform

The relativistic account, the frame it requires, and the frame it never shows.
The resolution offered

A frame where the water is at rest

  • The Lorentz transformation is applied in the frame where the water is stationary.
  • In that frame the starlight arrives already aberrated, refracts by Snell, and reads 20.55″.
  • That frame is the one in which the Earth carrying the water is at rest.
  • The transform's lineage runs Voigt (1887) → Lorentz → Einstein, built inside aether theory to make every experiment come out as if the Earth were at rest.
A device engineered to produce nulls, later presented as the discovery that explains them.
The omission

What the moving frame predicts

For 155 years, published treatments stop at the water-at-rest frame. Pauli notes the moving case introduces difficulties and declines to show it.
Water at rest
15.45″
reads 20.55″ ✓
Water in motion
11.88″
reads 15.80″ ✗
Measured
20.55″
air and water
We are supposed to be the moving frame. It is the frame that fails.
The trap

Frames must agree

  • Reciprocity between frames is the backbone of the theory. It is how invariance is validated.
  • Every frame must agree on what the instrument reads.
  • Two different answers means you can distinguish between frames.
The escape, and its cost

"We do not live in that frame, so it does not matter" abandons the framework entirely. If the analysis is allowed to disagree with the lab, invariance is gone.

Either the moving frame reproduces 20.55″, or the theory distinguishes frames. Pick one.
Closing the escape hatch

How fast is light in water?

Faster · n·c
1.33 c
superluminal, refuted by Foucault 1850
Slower · c/n
0.75 c
measured, still and moving water
"Cannot be known"
—
measured for 150 years
Feed the real speed into the naive aberration formula and it returns 27.33″. To get 15″ from that formula you need 1.33 c, which is not a speed but the refractive index in disguise.
The 15.45″ comes from refraction, not from a light speed. That is why the speed does not move it.
Methods note

A degree of arc is a length

  • A nautical mile is defined as one arc-minute. One degree of latitude is 69.08 statute miles.
  • Vector triangles carry whatever the vector carries: velocity, force, miles, arc.
  • adjacent = hypotenuse × cos(θ) is the definition of cosine, valid at every angle.
3959 × cos(89°) = 69.09 3959 × cos(60°) = 1979.5 3959 × cos(12°) = 3872.5 3959 × cos(41°) = 2988.0
FIGURE
Spreadsheet: 90 rows,
planar angle → celestial arc
Ninety rows, one formula, every angle correct. Not a special case.
Section Four

The Verdict

Every model of light propagation, measured against one instrument reading.
All treatments

Measured against 20.55″

ModelFrameθ_intReadsVerdict
Emission—15.45″20.55″needs 1.33 c
Undulatory / Snell—27.33″36.35″fails by n²
Special Relativitywater in motion11.88″15.80″never measured
Special Relativitywater at rest15.45″20.55″matches
Earth at restsingle frame15.45″20.55″matches
Only the frame where the water, and the Earth carrying it, is at rest reproduces the measurement.
Conclusion

There is no model that gives 20.55″ twice for a moving Earth

However you treat light, as corpuscles, waves, or packets of energy, no propagation model predicts 20.55″ in air and 20.55″ in water for an Earth in motion.
  • Relativity reaches the measured value only in the water-at-rest frame.
  • That frame is dynamically the stationary Earth.
  • Emission theory fails on speed. The wave theory fails on angle. The moving frame fails on both.
If experiment is to match theory, the Earth must be dynamically at rest.
Interactive

Run every prediction yourself

Switch to Simulation in the toggle at the top of this page. Every theory in these slides is live: pick the model, pick the frame, and read the predicted angle against Airy's lab-frame value.
  • Theory — emission, undulatory, Klinkerfues, Snell, SR, Pauli, Rosser, geocentric.
  • Frame — water at rest against water in motion.
  • Show — calibrated reading, raw air scale, internal angle, or micrometer drift in µm.
Same numbers, same scale factor, no hidden steps.
Sources

Primary references

  • Airy, G. B. (1871). On a supposed alteration in the amount of astronomical aberration of light. MNRAS, 31, 121–123.
  • Bradley, J. (1728). A new apparent motion discovered in the fixed stars. Phil. Trans., 35, 637–661.
  • Michelson, A. A. & Morley, E. W. (1887). On the relative motion of the Earth and the luminiferous ether. Am. J. Sci., 34, 333–345.
  • Foucault, L. (1850). Méthode générale pour mesurer la vitesse de la lumière. C. R. Acad. Sci., 30, 551–560.
  • Klinkerfues, W. (1867). Die Aberration der Fixsterne nach der Wellentheorie.
  • Pauli, W. (1921). Theory of Relativity, §36γ, eq. (311b).
  • Rosser, W. G. V. (1964). Introduction to the Theory of Relativity, §4.4, eq. (4.30).
